phrases.intent_en: Ask what fish is freshest today
phrases.text_local: Quel poisson est le plus frais aujourd'hui ?
phrases.pronunciation: kel pwah-SOHN eh luh ploo FRAY oh-zhoor-DWEE
phrases.cultural_note: Honfleur's fish market receives catches from local boats working the Seine estuary and English Channel. The catch varies daily — sole, turbot, sea bass (bar), and shrimp (crevettes grises) are local specialities.

responses.english: The sole is very fresh today.
responses.phonetic: lah SOHL eh treh FRESH oh-zhoor-DWEE
responses.french: La sole est très fraîche aujourd'hui.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a small portion of grey shrimps
phrases.text_local: Je voudrais une portion de crevettes grises, s'il vous plaît.
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh voo-DRAY oon por-SYOHN duh kruh-VET GREEZ seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: Grey shrimps (crevettes grises) from the Norman coast are tiny, intensely flavoured, and eaten cold — often with bread, butter, and a squeeze of lemon. They are sold in small cups and eaten on the quayside by locals and tourists alike.

phrases.intent_en: Ask the fishmonger to clean and fillet a fish
phrases.text_local: Pourriez-vous vider et lever les filets de ce poisson, s'il vous plaît ?
phrases.pronunciation: poor-YAY-voo vee-DAY ay luh-VAY lay fee-LAY duh suh pwah-SOHN seel voo PLAY
phrases.cultural_note: French fishmongers (poissonniers) will fillet and clean fish for you at no extra charge. This service is standard practice and highly valued — it takes skill and a good knife.

responses.english: Yes, I'll prepare it for you.

phrases.intent_en: Ask what time the market closes
phrases.text_local: À quelle heure ferme le marché aux poissons ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UR FAIRM luh mar-SHAY oh pwah-SOHN
phrases.cultural_note: The fish market is typically busiest in the morning between 7am and 1pm. Stock sells out quickly — arriving after noon means limited choice. Saturdays tend to be the largest market days in Honfleur.

phrases.intent_en: Ask for a recommendation for cooking sole
phrases.text_local: Comment vous conseillez-vous de préparer la sole normande à la maison ?
phrases.pronunciation: koh-MAHN voo kohn-SAY-yay-VOO duh pray-pah-RAY lah SOL nor-MAHND ah lah may-ZOHN
phrases.cultural_note: Sole normande is classically prepared à la meunière — dusted in flour, sautéed in butter, and finished with lemon and parsley. Many fishmongers are happy to give cooking advice. Buying fish from the source in Honfleur is a memorable experience.

responses.english: In butter, with a bit of lemon.
responses.phonetic: oh BUHR, ah-VEK uhn puh duh see-TROHN
responses.french: Au beurre, avec un peu de citron.
